name: Model Size ↔ Memory
expansion: Memory Footprint Calculation for Quantized LLMs
oneliner: Memory (bytes) = params $\times$ bits_per_param / 8, plus overhead for scales, KV-cache, and non-quantized components.
explanation: To estimate a model's memory footprint, multiply the parameter count by the bytes per parameter for your chosen format. A 70-billion parameter model in 16-bit precision needs about 140 GB, while the same model in 4-bit quantization needs roughly 35 GB. Real usage is higher because embedding and normalization layers are typically kept in 16-bit, scale factors add overhead, and the KV cache grows with sequence length and batch size. For a 70B model, the KV cache alone can reach 2.5 GB per sequence at 4096 tokens.
fundamentals: Weight memory = P $\times$ b / 8. Scale overhead = P $\times$ (s_bits / g) / 8. KV-cache = 2 $\times$ layers $\times$ kv_heads $\times$ head_dim $\times$ seq_len $\times$ batch $\times$ kv_bytes. Practical: RTX 4090 (24 GB) fits 7B at fp16, 13B at int8, ~33B at int4. A100-80GB fits 70B at int8. H100 at 3.35 TB/s: 70B int4 $\approx$ 55 tok/s, 70B fp16 $\approx$ 14 tok/s.
sources: Pope et al., 'Efficiently Scaling Transformer Inference,' MLSys 2023, arXiv:2211.05102
